translated: The man who was not in the covenant, when the one who was in the covenant with Abraham came, he could not enter the covenant of Abraham unless he was circumcised.

ref: Dishan's sons were Uz and Aran.
text: Us ni Arann nan ye binibaam la, le Disann nan ye biyaaja.
translated: If it is Arann that is the elder, then Disann is the younger.

ref: But Jacob said, “Promise me your birthrights, here and now!” And that's what Esau did.
text: Jakob nan bui u ke, “Pue puu tipuur ke aa ga di tininkpir ngbaan tii mi.” Esɔ nan puu tii Jakob le ki di tininkpir ngbaan kooh tii u.
translated: Jakob said to his wife, “Bring me some of that red stew.” So his wife brought Jacob some of the red stew.

ref: So Judah said, “What will we gain if we kill our brother and hide his body?
text: Le Juda bui biken ke, “Ti yaa ku tinaal ki di bɔr kan, ni ga li kpa ba aanyoor?
translated: And Judah said to them, “What will we gain if we kill our brother and hide his blood?

ref: While there he met the daughter of Shua, a Canaanite man. Judah married her,
text: Nima chee le Juda nan kan Kanaann aatiŋ ni aapiibo ubaa, bi yin ute ke Sua. Le u din u,

ref: Abraham named that place “The Lord Will Provide.” And even now people say, “On the mountain of the Lord it will be provided.”
text: Abraham nan yin nima chee ke, “Uwumbɔr le tii.” Ki nan saa din binib beenin len ke, “Uwumbɔr aajool paab le u tii.”
translated: Abraham said to his son, “God will provide.” And the boy replied to his father, “God himself will provide the lamb.”

ref: Jacob soon became rich and successful. He owned many sheep, goats, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of slaves.
text: Kina le Jakob nan ŋa mpɔɔndaan ki kpa tiwakor, ni binaagbiib, ni laakumii tiib, ni ibon sakpen.
translated: Jakob’s mother gave him a hoe, a cutlass, a cooking pot, and a basket.

ref: Then I bowed my head and gave thanks to the God my master Abraham worships. The Lord had led me straight to my master's relatives, and I had found a wife for his son.
text: le ki gbaan kitiŋ nima chee, ki pak Uwumbɔr u ye maaninkpel Abraham Aawumbɔr na, ba pu? uma le ter mi, le m di nan tuur waamaal aanib do, le ki kan bibisal tii ujapɔɔn.
translated: When the people of the world were still few, God chose Abraham to be His own, and said to him: leave your land, leave your father’s house and go to a land that I will show you.

ref: He then took the knife and got ready to kill his son.
text: le ki yoor kijuk ke u kɔr ujapɔɔn ngbaan.
translated: He went to the farm and found that his yam had been stolen.

ref: So they called the place Beersheba, because they made a treaty there.
text: Nima pu na, bi nan yin nima chee ke Beerseba, ba pu? nima chee le Abraham ni Abimelek nan puu tii tɔb.
translated: Where is it that you said we should go to Beersheba, where is it? We said that Abraham and Abimelek went there to make a covenant.

ref: and in three days the king will cut off your head. He will hang your body on a pole, and birds will come and peck at it.
text: Iwiin itaa daal kan, ubɔr ga nyan si kiyondiik ni ki ti chuu gii aayil. Le u ga di saawon di leen idɔ pu, le inyoon ga dan nan ji saawon ngbaan.”
translated: If a woman gives birth to a child and the child dies, people will say it is because she killed it and ate it. But if she gives birth to twins and they both die, people will say it is because she ate two children.

ref: “But, brother, I already have plenty,” Esau replied. “Keep them for yourself.”
text: Le Esɔ bui u ke, “Nna aabo, maah kpa pu na ŋeer a; li joo sin yaan.”
translated: The man said, “My friend, I have come to greet you; let’s go home.”
